authoritative_sources.title: 2017 NAICS Manual, 5412 Accounting, Tax Preparation, Bookkeeping, and Payroll Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The U.S. Census Bureau is the authoritative NAICS publisher. NAICS 5412 establishes that this industry includes auditing accounting records, designing accounting systems, preparing financial statements, developing budgets, preparing tax returns, payroll processing, bookkeeping, and billing.

authoritative_sources.title: Professional Responsibilities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA is the national professional body for CPAs. Its public professional responsibilities guidance summarizes Code of Professional Conduct duties for integrity, objectivity, due care, competence, conflicts, client confidentiality, and the public interest.

authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Statement on Quality Management Standards No. 1
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA SQMS No. 1 is an official quality-management standard for firms. It requires firms to design, implement, and operate quality management, including resource, engagement-performance, information, communication, monitoring, and remediation considerations.

authoritative_sources.title: Schedule Assessment Guide: Best Practices for Project Schedules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral planning, scheduling, or resource-allocation references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO is the U.S. congressional audit, evaluation, and investigative agency. Its Schedule Assessment Guide is a public framework for schedule logic, sequencing, dependencies, resource constraints, critical paths, and schedule risk that supports planning discipline without replacing accounting-practice standards or approval rules.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Schedule logic, dependency, resource, and risk assessment

authoritative_sources.title: Say I do to engagement letters
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA practice-management guidance explains that engagement letters establish scope, duration, prices or rates, relationship boundaries, records management, confidentiality, billing and payment terms, dispute resolution, and scope-change handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Statements on Standards for Accounting and Review Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA SSARS are recognized standards for preparation, compilation, and review engagements. They establish engagement documentation, management responsibility, reporting, and review boundaries relevant when recurring accounting practice work touches financial statement services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Financial-statement service documentation and signoff boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Office of Professional Responsibility and Circular 230
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The IRS Office of Professional Responsibility has delegated authority over tax practitioner conduct and discipline, and Circular 230 governs practice before the IRS through mandatory competence, diligence, and conduct rules.

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1215, Audit Documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB is the regulator for audits of public companies and broker-dealers. AS 1215 establishes audit-documentation expectations and completion discipline that this planning lane must treat as adjacent audit-work evidence and not override.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audit evidence and workpaper boundary enforcement

authoritative_sources.title: QC 1000, A Firm's System of Quality Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB QC 1000 is the SEC-approved public-company audit firm quality-control standard, effective December 15, 2026, and provides regulator-issued resource, engagement-performance, monitoring, and quality-risk boundaries when practice-management planning touches audit delivery.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, planning horizon, client roster, service model, engagement scope, close period, delivery commitments, and required tenant context before creating any schedule or allocation recommendation.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Engagement, workpaper, and client-readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Check engagement letters, service-level commitments, close checklists, reconciliation inventories, PBC or document-request status, workpaper support, reviewer notes, and confidentiality restrictions.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare client demand against preparer availability, reviewer and partner queues, close-calendar timing, approved service levels, utilization or realization posture, billing constraints, and client-document dependencies.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Vendor-neutral planning, scheduling, or resource-allocation references

workflow_stages.name: Schedule, allocation, and priority recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Generate recommendation-only close-calendar sequencing, staffing mix, client-priority, and deferral options with explicit assumptions, shortfall logic, source records, and downstream client-delivery impacts.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Vendor-neutral planning, scheduling, or resource-allocation references

workflow_stages.name: Approval, confidentiality, and professional-judgment escalation
workflow_stages.description: When engagement scope, client consent, confidentiality, quality review, accounting-policy judgment, tax, legal, assurance, contract, billing, or final approval authority is needed, stop deterministic completion and route to the correct owner.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: engagement letters, service-level commitments, contract addenda, client communication rules, and approval matrices
monthly, quarterly, and year-end close calendars; close checklists; delivery calendars; PBC trackers; and client-priority trackers
reconciliation inventories, workpaper indexes, bank-feed support, trial balances, journal-entry support, flux review notes, exception logs, and reporting-package readiness records
preparer, reviewer, manager, and partner assignments; availability calendars; utilization plans; WIP and realization signals; capacity reports; and staffing plans
quality-review checklists, reviewer notes, signoff queues, retention expectations, confidentiality restrictions, and document-access rules
practice allocation recommendations, close-priority recommendations, constraint logs, capacity-risk escalation notes, approval-required tradeoff notes, and adjacent-specialist routing notes
systems_of_record: practice management or professional-services automation system
ERP, GL, and client accounting workflow systems
document management, workpaper, and client collaboration repository
resource-planning, scheduling, and availability calendar system
timekeeping, billing, WIP, and realization system
tenant policy library, confidentiality controls, engagement repository, and approval matrix

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for accounting-practice workload planning
organize authoritative close, engagement, workpaper, client, staffing, billing, and review records into recommendation-only outputs
recommend close-calendar sequencing, client-priority posture, staffing mix, deferrals, and allocation changes under documented constraints without claiming final approval
prepare escalation packets for partner, engagement owner, quality review, tax, legal, advisory, assurance, delivery management, or account owners
escalate: missing or stale engagement scope, service level, close-calendar, workpaper, reviewer, client-document, confidentiality, or approval records
tenant policy and public baseline conflict on confidentiality, documentation, quality review, engagement scope, or professional judgment
the request requires final staffing, budget, billing, realization, contract, tax, legal, accounting-policy, assurance, review, compilation, filing, or partner approval
systems of record materially disagree on work status, remaining effort, client readiness, reviewer ownership, or deadline commitments
client rights, consent, or confidentiality restrictions determine the answer
refuse: final staffing, budget, billing, realization, fee, contract, or client-commitment approval requests
legal advice, tax advice, accounting-policy judgment, audit opinion, review, compilation, attestation, filing, or partner-level signoff requests
record sharing, queue reassignment, or client work release when confidentiality, consent, engagement scope, or authorization is missing
generic headcount or productivity answers that ignore accounting-practice artifacts, workpaper support, engagement scope, quality review, or client commitments

domain_failure_modes: Treating accounting, tax, and audit services as generic resource planning and ignoring engagement scope, close calendars, workpaper support, client readiness, and reviewer queues.
Producing deterministic allocation despite missing workpaper support, client service commitments, confidentiality restrictions, or authoritative system records.
Overriding quality-review, reviewer-note, client-document, or workpaper gates to preserve a close date or utilization target.
Drifting into partner approval, billing approval, contract change, accounting-policy judgment, tax advice, legal advice, audit opinion, compilation, review, or attestation authority.
Allowing public professional baselines and tenant policy conflicts on confidentiality, documentation, or approval flow to collapse into confident synthesis.
Sharing or moving client records across teams, third parties, or tenants without confirmed authorization, client consent, and retrieval controls.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness
Escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State board, jurisdiction, and engagement-specific professional rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: AICPA, IRS, PCAOB, GAO, and NAICS sources provide public baselines, but state board rules, engagement terms, client consent, and service-specific standards can vary. The package treats those details as tenant-supplied runtime prerequisites and escalates instead of guessing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific close calendars, approval thresholds, and client tiering rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources establish professional and workflow boundaries, but each tenant must supply close calendars, service model, priority tie-breaks, approval matrix, offshore or third-party sharing rules, WIP and realization policy, and retrieval mappings for deterministic planning.

notes: Authoritative tenant records are the designated practice management, close management, ERP, GL, workpaper, document-management, scheduling, timekeeping, billing, quality-review, policy, and engagement systems constrained by the public baseline above.

The lane stops before tax advice, legal advice, accounting-policy judgment, audit opinion, compilation or review signoff, filing approval, contract change, billing or realization approval, confidentiality exception, and final staffing or partner approval.

No unresolved ambiguity is blocking for this validated package because jurisdiction, tenant, engagement, client-consent, and approval gaps are explicitly handled as runtime prerequisites and escalation conditions.
